name: The Drill Platform
description: The open-air timber platform surrounding the top of the bore hole, forty feet above grade on the tower's north face, where the drilling machinery and operating equipment are mounted. The Well_Drill_Torque gauge is bolted to the main frame here, its needle sitting at 62 in the gas-lamp light.
atmosphericDetails: The metallic screech of the jammed driveshaft carries down to the street every time the rotary table is jogged — a sound that has been drawing onlookers for two hours.
Fine grey rock dust coats every horizontal surface, including the blueprint sheet Benezette has pinned to the frame with a steel spike.
The brake collar on the rotary table has a fresh scratch mark on its adjustment bolt — visible only in direct lantern light at close range.
